channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Hello Kitty Island Adventure City Town DLC Finally Set for April
item.content:encoded:
Developer Sunblink has announced that the City Town DLC for cozy sandbox Hello Kitty Island Adventure finally has a release date, and it's bringing a new friend and much more with it in April.

As revealed in a brand new trailer, Hello Kitty Island Adventure's City Town DLC will arrive across all platforms on April 16th. The studio, along with IP holder Sanrio, describes the DLC as taking place in a "rainbow metropolis" in which you'll befriend classic Sanrio character and adorable bunny Usahana.

She'll serve as your guide through the titular City Town, and she'll also assist in the renovation of the brand new Imagination Cafe, where you can create your very own treats to "delight your Sanrio friends", as Sunblink says. You'll also be able to customize the cafe and add your own designs.

The new City Town area, according to Sanrio (via Variety's report), promises over 30 hours of new stuff to experience, as well as more than 90 quests to uncover "both above and beneath the city".

As well as City Town and the new cafe, you'll also be able to open up seven new shops alongside your Sanrio friends, including Tuxedosam and Kuromi. New visitors will also arrive courtesy of Usahana, and you'll have the chance to "prepare cozy apartments inside the Rainbow Tower to welcome them in style".

All of that is on top of a "mysterious cameo guest" that Sunblink is teasing, although the studio naturally stops short of revealing exactly who it is. You'll have to wait and see when the DLC comes out, I guess.

If you're not familiar with Hello Kitty Island Adventure, it's a cozy game reminiscent of titles like Animal Crossing: New Horizons. It is, as you'd expect, replete with Sanrio characters, and it incorporates more exploration and puzzles than many of its peers, as the name suggests.

The game has struck a chord with players; after launching across Switch and PC last year, it had managed to top 500,000 sales by March, and that's without counting August's PS5 release, naturally.

Hello Kitty Island Adventure is available right now on PC, PlayStation 5, and both Switch 2 and Switch. The game's City Town DLC launches on April 16th.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph
